name: dotCMS Headless Content Management API
what_it_does: Open-source Java-based headless CMS with REST and GraphQL API — enabling enterprise teams to manage content, digital assets, and multi-site publishing with content types, page builder, workflow, and versioning, delivering headless content to any frontend via REST Content API or GraphQL, with an on-premises or cloud option and strong Elasticsearch-powered search.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via dotCMS REST Content API for Next.js or React frontend integration
Managing content types and pages via dotCMS REST API for content operations automation
Building multi-site content networks with shared content via dotCMS API
Integrating dotCMS content search via Elasticsearch-powered query API for search use cases
Managing digital assets and transformations via dotCMS Assets API for media pipeline
not_for: Small teams needing simple CMS (dotCMS Java stack adds operational complexity)
Organizations needing SaaS-only with no server management
best_when: A Java-ecosystem enterprise team needs an open-source headless CMS with multi-site support, content workflows, and strong search — especially for financial services, healthcare, or government where open-source auditability and on-premises deployment are required.
avoid_when: You don't have Java infrastructure expertise, need a simple headless CMS, or prefer SaaS-only without server management.

auth.notes: API token (JWT) for REST Content API. OAuth2 for user-delegated access. Bearer token auth. Role-based access via dotCMS permissions system. Webhook for content publish events. REST Content API for headless delivery. GraphQL API (contrib module). Multi-site with site-specific access controls. SAML SSO via dotCMS SAML plugin. On-premises or dotCMS Cloud hosting.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community: open source self-hosted, free with community support
pricing.paid_tiers: dotCMS Cloud from $1,500/month; Enterprise license from $50,000/year; contact sales
pricing.notes: Miami company. Founded 2003. Bootstrapped. Open source under GPL license. 500+ enterprise customers. Used by Estee Lauder, Comcast, UCLA, American Cancer Society. Strong in financial services, healthcare, and government. Java-based (Spring Boot). Elasticsearch for content search. React-based drag-and-drop page builder. Multi-site and multi-language native support. Active GitHub community (10,000+ stars). Cloud-hosted dotCMS option for managed deployment. Open source makes it auditable and customizable.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: infrastructure-controlled; Cloud: enterprise limits per contract

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST Content API + GraphQL. JWT token + OAuth2 auth. Good documentation with API examples. Webhook for content events. Open source codebase for debugging. Java developer required for customization. Elasticsearch-backed search provides powerful content queries. Self-hosted adds infrastructure management. Community support for open source; paid support for Enterprise.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads fully idempotent. Content type reads idempotent by variable name. Asset reads idempotent by UUID. Mutations use content identifier for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Java developer required for dotCMS customization and plugin development
Open source self-hosting requires Java server infrastructure management
GraphQL is contrib module — may lag core REST API in feature parity
Multi-site configuration adds complexity — content access is site-scoped
SAML SSO requires separate plugin installation and configuration

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2 compliant. Self-hosted for full data residency control. JWT + OAuth2 auth. Open-source codebase enables security audit (GPL license). HIPAA BAA for healthcare cloud customers. Self-hosted eliminates cloud data residency concerns. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. Content permissions granular per role and site. Regular security patches via open source community. Java ecosystem security scanning tools applicable.
